ogTitle: Drome vs Drummond Cove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Drome and Drummond Cove.
heading: Drome vs Drummond Cove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Drome, Western Australia and Drummond Cove, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Drome has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Drummond Cove (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Drome or Drummond Cove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Drome has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Drummond Cove scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Drome scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Drummond Cove scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Drome or Drummond Cove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Drome has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
